task.prompt: Write a function `clamp(value, low, high)` that restricts a number to be within the range [low, high].

gold_spec.goal: Clamp value to the range [low, high]. Raise ValueError if low > high.
preconditions: low <= high, otherwise ValueError is raised
postconditions: if low <= value <= high then result == value
if value < low then result == low
if value > high then result == high
edge_cases: low == high — any value clamps to that single point
value exactly equals low or high — returned unchanged

known_weakness.description: The task says 'restricts to range [low, high]' but does not say what happens when low > high. A naive spec might produce postconditions like 'low <= result <= high' which become contradictory (unsatisfiable) when low > high. The Distinguisher must flag that the spec needs to either add a precondition (low <= high) or specify error behavior for the inverted-range case.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: no handling of the low > high case — postconditions become contradictory
